page: Microsoft Azure ND128isr_GB300_v6 is an isolated virtual machine belonging to the NDISRGB300V6 server family. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs built on the arm64 CPU architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 864.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.75 GB of RAM per core, and includes a single onboard GPU.

For high-capacity data requirements, this server is bundled with 16,408 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable. It supports Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) networking, which enables low-latency, high-throughput communication between nodes in clustered environments. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

Given the combination of 128 dedicated arm64 vCPUs, a dedicated GPU, and over 16 TB of local storage, this server is designed for resource-intensive workloads. It is suitable for large-scale database systems, GPU-accelerated machine learning tasks, and high-performance computing applications requiring isolated hardware. The high resource density of storage, memory, and compute provides qualitative cost efficiency for clustered enterprise deployments.
description: Microsoft Azure ND128isr_GB300_v6 is an isolated, RDMA-capable virtual machine from the NDISRGB300V6 family, featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs on the arm64 architecture. Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server is equipped with 864.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.75 GB per core, and includes a single GPU. It features a substantial 16,408 GB of local storage with premium storage capabilities, making it highly suitable for data-intensive operations. Designed for high-performance clustering, its RDMA support facilitates low-latency node communication. This hardware profile is optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads, large-scale databases, and isolated high-performance computing tasks that require massive local storage capacity and dedicated processing power.
